license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.iu2ceye
title: Density Gradient Fractionation of RNA from RNA-SIP Experiments
author: Julie Huber

steps_raw.instruction: Label 15ml tubes with sample names. Do not run more then 6-8 samples. Always include at least one No RNA control.
Add 5.1ml of CsTFA. Using a 5ml syringe and 0.2um filter, add 5ml of CsTFA to each tube. Then, using a 1ml syringe and 0.2um filter add the 0.1ml.
Add 185ul formamide to each tube. Formamide should be stored in small aliquots in -20C freezer to decrease degradation.

Add 1ml minus volume of 750ng of RNA of Gradient Salt Solution. E.g. If you are going to add 20ul of RNA, then add 980ul of Gradient Salt Solution. Recipe for solution can be found in Neufeld et al. 2010, JoVe article.
Add 750ng of RNA. Can add 500-750ng, but no lower.
Mix gently by inverting. DO NOT vortex.
Set refractometer to nd-TC setting. Calibrate using MilliQ water.

Using 75ul from each tube, measure Refractive Index (RI) on refractometer. Each tube should measure 1.3729±0.0002. If RI is too high or too low, adjust using CsTFA (if too low) or Gradient Salt solution (too high). Add 50-100ul to adjust. Then mix and re-measure. DO NOT proceed until each tube is within range. Also measure pure CsTFA each time to check if there are changes in density.

Use 5ml serological pipette to fill each 5ml Beckman centrifuge tube. Fill to bottom of neck. Do not overfill! Place black caps on top. Note, Beckman tubes should be rinsed with 70% ethanol and dried ahead of time. Determine mass of each filled tube on scale. It is easiest if you place the tube in a small beaker before measuring. Make sure each tube has a comparable mass.
Pair up tubes with similar masses and load into rotor. MAKE SURE IT IS BALANCED. Place gold caps on top of tubes, then thread in the caps. Use ratchet to tighten.
Place rotor in ultracentrifuge. Run @37,000 rpm, 20C, for >60 hours. I run for 64 hours. Make sure program has NO BRAKE for deceleration option.

Ahead of time, do the following: -Make 70% ethanol, place in freezer -Aliquot 100% isopropanol into 50ml tube, place in freezer -Make ~50ml of colored MilliQ water with food dye or Resazurin (I use 500ul) -Autoclave tubing for low-flow pump -Set-up ring stand with test-tube clamp -Sterilize and label 1.5 tubes for each sample. Label 1-12. Can use label maker. -Place tubes in racks. I do 24 tubes per rack (2 samples)
Take rotor out of ultracentrifuge, unload tubes and place in black rack.
Set-up pump and ring-stand. Make sure pump is set to 0.42ml/min.
Prime tubing with colored MilliQ water.
Screw needle on end of tubing, prime again.
Place 5ml tube in clamp and ready 1.5ml tubes. Make sure tube 1 is the FIRST tube filled, this is the heaviest.
Puncture the bottom of 5ml tube with needle.
Place tube rack underneath 5ml tube. Make sure first tube has cap open.
Puncture top of 5ml tube with the primed needle attached to tubing.
Set timer for 12 minutes. Simultaneously, press start on pump and timer.10. Shift rack every minute to begin collection into new 1.5ml tube. There should be about ~420ul per tube.
Measure and record the RI for each fraction. The middle fractions, tubes 6-7 should be ~1.3729. If this is not the case, may need to re-run.
Repeat fractionation for each sample.
Add 1 volume of ice-cold 100% Isopropanol to each tube. Mix gently by inverting. Incubate in freezer for >30min. Can do overnight, although we have not tried this.
Spin tubes for 25min in microcentrifuge in COLD ROOM to precipitate RNA.
Pipet off liquid. Try not to touch bottom, disturb the pellet.
Add 150ul of 70% ethanol.
